class="docs-module__4gIjsq__tableContainer MuiBox-root css-i169fm"><h2 id="overview">Overview</h2>
<p>B2C API is used to make payments from a Business to Customers' number also known as Bulk Disbursements.</p>
<h2 id="how-it-works">How It Works</h2>
<ol>
<li>The Merchant (Partner) prepares and sends a payment request to the M-PESA B2C API endpoint.</li>
<li>The API Management Platform validates, authorizes, and authenticates the request, forwards it to M-PESA, and sends an acknowledgment to the Merchant.</li>
<li>M-PESA validates the request and initiator details, then processes the transaction.</li>
<li>M-PESA sends the transaction response to the Merchant via the specified callback URL.</li>
<li>The Customer receives an SMS notification confirming the payment</li>
</ol>
<blockquote>
<p><strong>Note:</strong> To go live and use the API in production, you must apply for a Bulk Disbursement Account and obtain a B2C Short Code or you convert your existing pay bill/till number to one account(can receive and disburse). Apply for the Bulk Disbursement Account/one account here <a href="https://www.safaricom.co.ke/business/sme/m-pesa-payment-solutions">https://www.safaricom.co.ke/business/sme/m-pesa-payment-solutions</a>.</p>
</blockquote>
<h2 id="getting-started">Getting Started</h2>
<h3 id="prerequisites">Prerequisites</h3>
<ol>
<li>Create a Daraja Account on <a href="https://daraja.safaricom.co.ke/">Safaricom Developer Portal</a>.</li>
<li>Create a sandbox app in the portal to get API credentials.</li>
<li>Retrieve Consumer Key & Consumer Secret from your sandbox app on <a href="https://daraja.safaricom.co.ke/dashboard/myapps">My Apps</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Test data:</strong> Available on the simulator section.</li>
<li><strong>Initiator Username:</strong> This is the API operator's username as set on the portal when the user was created. For Sandbox users, the username is already created and assigned to them and is available on the test credentials page as InitiatorName.</li>
<li><strong>Initiator Password:</strong> This is the password assigned to the API operator after being created by the Business Administrator. For Sandbox users, this is available as InitiatorPassword on the test credentials page.<br>
<strong>Note:</strong> The password should be limited to specific special characters such as #, &, %, and $. Other characters might cause issues, and the password may not be accepted. For example, using a <code>(</code> or <code>)</code> character will not be accepted. In addition, @ is not a special character on M-Pesa; it is treated as a normal character.</li>
<li><strong>Public Key Certificate:</strong> This certificate is used to encrypt the Initiator's plaintext password for use in API calls. It is provided for both Sandbox and Production clients on the portal. You need to encrypt the password using your API language or use the test credentials section, which offers the capability to encrypt your password.</li>
<li><strong>Live M-PESA B2C shortcode, Paybill/till number:</strong> For Go Live, you need a shortcode or number that can receive and disburse funds (one account) with Business Admin/Manager operators created.</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="good-to-know">Good to Know</h3>
<p>This API is asynchronous.</p>
<h4 id="get-auth-token">Get Auth Token</h4>
<p>Devs gather here! You will first generate an access token to authenticate you to make the API call. See below generate access token <a href="https://daraja.safaricom.co.ke/dashboard/apis?api=Authorization">API</a> here. We’ve also automated this on the simulate request section.</p>
<h2 id="integration-steps">Integration Steps</h2>
<h3 id="sequence-diagram">Sequence Diagram</h3>
<p><img src="/docs/images/business-to-customer-sequence-diagram.png" alt="business-to-customer-sequence-diagram!" title="business-to-customer-sequence-diagram"></p>
<h3 id="use-cases">Use Cases</h3>
<ul>
<li>
<p><strong>Salary Payments</strong><br>
Disbursement of employee wages from businesses to individual customer wallets or bank accounts.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Cashback Payments</strong><br>
Automated refunds or rewards where a portion of money spent by customers is returned to their wallets as part of loyalty or promotional programs.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Promotional Payments</strong><br>
Incentive payouts such as lottery winnings, betting rewards, or other campaign-based disbursements to customers.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Winnings</strong><br>
Payouts from contests, games, or competitions where participants receive monetary rewards.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Financial Institutions Withdrawal of Funds</strong><br>
Transfers initiated to move funds from a financial institution or digital wallet to a customer’s account or for cash-out purposes.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Loan Disbursements</strong><br>
Distribution of approved loan amounts from lenders directly to borrowers’ wallets or bank accounts.</p>
</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="request-body">Request Body</h3>
<pre><code class="language-json">{
  "OriginatorConversationID": "600997_Test_32et3241ed8yu",
  "InitiatorName": "testapi",
  "SecurityCredential": "RC6E9WDxXR4b9X2c6z3gp0oC5Th ==",
  "CommandID": "BusinessPayment",
  "Amount": "10",
  "PartyA": "600992",
  "PartyB": "254705912645",
  "Remarks": "remarked",
  "QueueTimeOutURL": "https://mydomain.com/path",
  "ResultURL": "https://mydomain.com/path",
  "Occassion": "ChristmasPay"
}
</code></pre>

<h2 id="how-to">How To</h2>
<p><strong>Access to the M-PESA Organization Portal and create Users</strong></p>
<p>The M-PESA organization portal is a platform designed for businesses and organizations to manage their financial transactions through the M-PESA services.</p>
<p>The portal offers various features for businesses, including:</p>
<ol>
<li>Transaction Management: Monitor and manage transactions, generate reports, and reconcile accounts.</li>
<li>Account Management: Manage multiple user accounts with different access levels within the organization.</li>
<li>Bulk Payments: Facilitate bulk payments to employees, suppliers, and other beneficiaries efficiently.</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Access to the M-PESA Organization Portal</strong></p>
<p><img src="/docs/images/org-portal.png" alt="org-portal!" title="org-portal"></p>
<p>To access the Organization portal (<a href="https://org.ke.m-pesa.com/orglogin.action">https://org.ke.m-pesa.com/orglogin.action</a>), you need to have a Business Administrator role created under your business short code (Pay Bill/ Till).</p>
<p>The first step before getting access, is to ensure the settlement option set to your short code is Bank via a Head Office application.</p>
<p>To make an application for a Head Office for your short code/Store Number kindly contact <a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a> where they will provide you with the forms to be filled.</p>
<p>After Head Office is created the <a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a> will also guide on the creation of the Business Administrator (username).</p>
<p><strong>How to login to M-PESA Organization Portal For the first time:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Launch the link from any of the browsers: <a href="https://org.ke.m-pesa.com">https://org.ke.m-pesa.com</a>.</li>
<li>Enter the Short code which is Bulk payment number.</li>
<li>Enter the Business Administrator username received</li>
</ol>
<p>To make an application for a Head Office for your short code/Store Number kindly contact <a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a> where they will provide you with the forms to be filled.
After Head Office is created, the <a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a> will also guide on the creation of the Business Administrator (username).</p>
<h2 id="how-to-login-to-m-pesa-organization-portal-for-the-first-time">How to login to M-PESA Organization Portal For the first time:</h2>
<ol>
<li>Launch the link from any of the browsers: <a href="https://org.ke.m-pesa.com">https://org.ke.m-pesa.com</a>.</li>
<li>Enter the Short code which is Bulk payment number.</li>
<li>Enter the Business Administrator username received on email.</li>
<li>Enter the first-time password received on the same email – the Password is case-sensitive (full stop is not part of the password).</li>
<li>Enter the Verification Code displayed then click login, on the Next Page.</li>
<li>Enter OTP to proceed to the change password page.</li>
<li>Enter the first-time password on email, then proceed to set a new password.</li>
<li>Confirm password, security question, and security answer one and two which are mandatory.</li>
<li>The Password must meet the password rules as instructed.</li>
<li>Submit to successfully activate the Business Administrator account.</li>
<li>After activation, you will only be entering your username, shortcode, and password verification code and entering the OTP received via SMS to log in.</li>
</ol>
<h2 id="account-types-in-c2b-organization">Account Types in C2B Organization</h2>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>MMF/WORKING/M-PESA ACCOUNT FOR ORGANIZATION</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>When an organization wants to make a business withdrawal, the funds are transferred to this account before the withdrawal request is made.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>UTILITY ACCOUNT</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Payments from customers are credited into the utility account.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>CHARGES PAID ACCOUNT</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>For payments received from customers, depending on the tariff, a charge is levied on the Organization or is split between the organization and the customer. The charges paid account is debited and always accrues a negative balance which has to be settled before an organization can make a withdrawal request.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>ORGANIZATION SETTLEMENT ACCOUNT</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>This account does the calculations for the organization operator when s/he initiates a revenue settlement. This account settles the charges paid account and then moves the balance from the Utility Account to the MMF account automatically. You will notice that the transaction type “Move funds from Utility to MMF” is no longer available as the revenue settlement process takes care of this.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ol>
<h2 id="various-roles-under-the-portal">Various Roles under the portal</h2>
<h3 id="business-administrator-role">Business Administrator role</h3>
<p>The Business Administrator role is crucial for managing and overseeing user activities within the portal.</p>
<p><strong>Responsibilities of a Business Administrator:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Create other system users and give them roles depending on what he/she wants them to do within the portal. The roles include:
<ul>
<li>A Business Web Operator who can initiate transactions, access statements, and check balance.</li>
<li>A business manager.</li>
<li>A Business Auditor – View Only/ Read Only Rights.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>Not able to view transactions.</li>
<li>User created by Safaricom (<a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a>).</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="business-manager">Business Manager</h3>
<p>The Business manager approves transactions, checks balances, and access statements.</p>
<p><strong>Responsibilities of a Business Manager:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>The user can View statements.</li>
<li>The user can initiate transactions.</li>
<li>The user can Approve/reject other transactions.</li>
<li>The user can withdraw funds from M-PESA.</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Proceed as below to create a business manager:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Log in the Organization portal as the Business administrator.</li>
<li>Select operators.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/select-operator.png" alt="select-operator!" title="select-operator"></p>
<ol start="3">
<li>Then click the add option.</li>
<li>It will take you to a new page where you will enter the username of the operator.</li>
<li>Select access channel as Web.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/access-channel.png" alt="access-channel!" title="access-channel"></p>
<ol start="6">
<li>Then select web profile default rule profile.</li>
<li>Then assign role...Business Manager and Set Restricted ORG API PASSWORD.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/assign-role.png" alt="assign-role!" title="assign-role"></p>
<ol start="8">
<li>Enter the KYC information of the operator then submit.</li>
</ol>
<h3 id="api-user-creation">API user creation</h3>
<p><strong>Proceed as below to create an API Operator:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Log in as the Business administrator.</li>
<li>Select operators.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/select-operator.png" alt="select-operator!" title="select-operator"></p>
<ol start="3">
<li>Then click the add option.</li>
<li>It will take you to a new page where you will enter the username of the API initiator.</li>
<li>Select access channel as API.</li>
<li>Then select web profile default rule profile.</li>
<li>Then assign role...look for the ORG B2C API initiator, Balance Query ORG API, Transaction Status query ORG API roles.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/assign-api-role.png" alt="assign-api-role!" title="assign-api-role"></p>
<ol start="8">
<li>Enter the KYC information of the operator then submit.</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Here is how to set the password for the API user:</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>Login as User with the Set Restrict Password Role i.e. The Business Manager.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/operator-management.png" alt="Operator Management!" title="Operator Management"></p>
<ol start="2">
<li>Click on My Functions, Then Operator Management.</li>
<li>Enter the API username to search after it populates the API user, At the end click on operations.</li>
<li>Then click on set password avoid such (@ or.) in the setting of this password.</li>
</ol>
<p><img src="/docs/images/set-password.png" alt="set-password.!" title="set-password."></p>
<h3 id="various-api-roles">Various API roles:</h3>
<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>API</th>
<th>API Role Assignment</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>B2C</td>
<td>ORG B2C API Initiator.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Business Pay Bill</td>
<td>Business Paybill Org API initiator.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Business Buy Goods</td>
<td>Business Buy Goods Org API initiator.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Transaction Status</td>
<td>Transaction Status query ORG API.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Reversals</td>
<td>Org Reversals Initiator.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Tax Remittance</td>
<td>Tax Remittance to KRA API.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Set Password role</td>
<td>Set Restricted ORG API PASSWORD.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h2 id="how-to-apply-for-a-live-pay-bill-numbertill-number-or-b2c-account">How to apply for a live pay bill number/till number or B2C Account</h2>
<p>Apply here: <a href="mailto:M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke">M-PESABusiness@Safaricom.co.ke</a></p>
<h2 id="support">Support</h2>
<h3 id="chatbot">Chatbot</h3>
<p>Developers can get instant responses using the Daraja Chatbot for both development and production support.</p>
<h3 id="production-issues--incident-management">Production Issues & Incident Management</h3>
<p>For production support and incident management, use:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Incident Management Page:</strong> Visit the <a href="https://daraja.safaricom.co.ke/dashboard/incidentmanagement">Incident Management</a> page.</li>
<li><strong>Email:</strong> Reach out to API support at <a href="mailto:apisupport@safaricom.co.ke">apisupport@safaricom.co.ke</a>.</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="frequently-asked-questions-faqs">Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)</h3>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Why am I not receiving callbacks on my B2C ResultURL?</strong><br>
Ensure your callback endpoint (ResultURL) is publicly accessible over the internet. Use a valid IP address or domain name, and confirm your server is reachable.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>What should I do if I encounter an invalid access token error?</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Check that your access token has not expired (tokens expire hourly).</li>
<li>Ensure you are using the correct consumer key and consumer secret to generate the token.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Can I use my actual B2C short code on the Daraja portal simulator?</strong><br>
No. The simulator supports only sandbox test short codes. Use your application or a REST client (e.g., Postman) for live short code testing.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>How do I know if the B2C request is successful?</strong><br>
The B2C API is asynchronous. You receive an acknowledgement immediately, but the actual transaction result is sent later to your specified ResultURL. A successful transaction has ResultCode 0.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Is there a test environment for B2C API?</strong><br>
Yes. Use the sandbox environment and test credentials provided on the Daraja developer portal. You can test via the simulator, your code, or any REST client.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Why am I getting a balance insufficient error, yet I have money in my B2C account?</strong><br>
B2C transactions debit the Utility account. Ensure sufficient funds are available in the Utility account, not just the MMF/Working account.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>How do I move funds from MMF/Working account to Utility account for B2C disbursement?</strong><br>
Log in to the M-PESA portal as a Business Web Operator:</p>
<ul>
<li>Go to My Functions > Initiate Transaction</li>
<li>Select "Business Services – Move Funds from MMF Account to Utility Account"</li>
<li>Enter required details and submit</li>
<li>Another operator must approve the transaction under My Tasks > Group Task<br>
This can also be automated via the B2B API.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Can I automate the funds transfer from MMF/Working account to Utility account?</strong><br>
Yes. Use the B2B API with CommandID <strong><em>BusinessTransferFromMMFToUtility</em></strong>. Contact <a href="mailto:apisupport@safaricom.co.ke">apisupport@safaricom.co.ke</a> for whitelisting.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>What are the minimum and maximum limits for B2C transaction amounts?</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Minimum: Ksh 10</li>
<li>Maximum per transaction: Ksh 250,000</li>
<li>Maximum customer account balance: Ksh 500,000</li>
<li>Maximum daily value: Ksh 500,000</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Which API role is required for B2C requests?</strong><br>
Assign the <strong><em>ORG B2C API initiator</em></strong> role to your API user (done by the Business Administrator).</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>My API user is showing pending active status; how do I activate it?</strong><br>
The API user must have their password set by a user with the Set <strong><em>Restricted ORG API PASSWORD</em></strong> role. Once set, the status changes to active.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Why am I getting "initiator information is invalid" error?</strong><br>
This means the API user credentials are incorrect. Check:</p>
<ul>
<li>Username</li>
<li>Encrypted password</li>
<li>Encryption algorithm/certificate (use the tool on the developer portal)</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Why am I getting "security credential is locked" error?</strong><br>
Multiple failed login attempts lock the API user password. The Business Administrator can unlock it on the M-PESA portal. Ensure the correct password is used.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>How do I resolve "Invalid API call as no apiproduct match found" error?</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>For production, ensure the B2C product is enabled on your Daraja app.</li>
<li>Verify you are calling the correct API endpoint.</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Do I need a passkey for B2C?</strong><br>
No. Passkey is only required for M-PESA Express/STK Push API.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Why is the B2C product missing when I try to Go Live with my pay bill?</strong><br>
Your pay bill may not have B2C permissions. You need a B2C or one account short code. Apply via <a href="https://m-pesaforbusiness.co.ke/">https://m-pesaforbusiness.co.ke/</a>.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Can I reverse a B2C transaction using the Reversal API?</strong><br>
No. B2C reversals are not supported via API and must be done manually on the M-PESA portal.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Why am I getting "Bad Request - Invalid InitiatorName" error?</strong><br>
Possible causes:</p>
<ul>
<li>Wrong Content-Type (should be application/json)</li>
<li>Incorrect parameter name</li>
<li>Invalid or missing InitiatorName value</li>
</ul>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Can I have both B2C and C2B on my Daraja application?</strong><br>
Yes, if both functionalities are enabled for your short code.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Which operator can create an API user?</strong><br>
Only the Business Administrator can create API users. Any web user with the Set Restricted ORG API PASSWORD role can set the password.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Do I need to generate the security credential for every B2C request?</strong><br>
No. You can reuse the same security credential for multiple requests.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Do I need to manually approve B2C requests initiated via API?</strong><br>
No. Manual approval is not required for API-initiated B2C requests.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Can I check B2C transaction status using the Transaction Status Query API?</strong><br>
Yes. Use the OriginatorConversationID or the M-PESA Receipt Number.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Is there an API for moving money from a pay bill number to a B2C short code?</strong><br>
Yes. Use the B2C Account Top Up API to move funds from your pay bill’s Working account to the B2C Utility account. See <a href="https://developer.safaricom.co.ke/APIs/B2CAccountTopUp">B2C Account Top Up API documentation</a>.</p>
</li>
</ol></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div style="padding-bottom:20px"></div><div class="MuiStack-root css-3vvq7q"><div class="MuiGrid2-root MuiGrid2-container MuiGrid2-direction-xs-row MuiGrid2-spacing-xs-3 css-177xvbk"><div class="MuiGrid2-root MuiGrid2-direction-xs-row MuiGrid2-grid-xs-12 MuiGrid2-grid-md-8 css-1vyu5pa"><p class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body1 css-8fsfud">Daraja 3.<span style="color:#E04445">0</span></p><p class="MuiTypography-root MuiTypography-body1 css-14gnh2z">Daraja 3.0 is a web platform that offers access to Safaricom and M-PESA APIs that creates a bridge for payment integration to web and mobile apps.
